conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_004. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
My tablet from order ORD-6031 won't charge anymore. I bought an extended warranty — is it covered?

Yes, your tablet is covered.  

Your extended warranty for the SlateTab Pro 11-inch is active until May 21, 2027 and includes repair-or-replace coverage, so a charging failure should be eligible. If you’d like, I can start a warranty claim for you now—please confirm that the issue is with the tablet itself (not just the cable/charger) and whether you’ve already tried a different outlet and cable.

Yes, please start a warranty claim.  

I’ve already tried multiple outlets and a different USB-C cable, and the tablet still won’t charge at all, so the issue is definitely with the tablet itself, not the charger.

I can file this under your extended warranty and it’s fully covered. The resolution will be a no-cost repair under your active extended warranty.  

Before I finalize it, here’s what will happen next:
- We’ll create a repair claim and provide you with instructions and a shipping label (if mail-in is required in your area).  
- A technician will inspect and repair the tablet; if it can’t be repaired, it may be replaced according to the warranty terms.  

Please confirm you want me to proceed with the repair claim under your warranty, and also let me know:
1) Is there any physical damage (cracks, dents, liquid exposure)?  
2) Can you still power it on at all, or is it completely dead now?

rules.expired_old: Expired >30 days: full-price repair or 25% off replacement.
rules.claim_limit: Max claims reached: paid repair only (40% of item price).
rules.recurring_defect: 2+ prior claims for same issue: automatic replacement.
rules.extended: Extended warranty covers after manufacturer period.

arguments.issue_description: Tablet (SlateTab Pro 11-inch) will not charge at all despite trying multiple outlets and a different USB-C cable. Issue confirmed to be with the tablet, not the charger.

conversation.content:
That all sounds good—yes, please proceed with the repair claim under my extended warranty.  

1) There’s no physical damage at all—no cracks, dents, or liquid exposure.  
2) It’s completely dead now and won’t power on, even when plugged in.

You’re all set—I’ve submitted the repair claim under your extended warranty, and it’s fully covered at no cost to you.  

Next steps:
1. You’ll receive an email shortly with your repair authorization and shipping instructions (including a prepaid label if mail-in is required in your area).  
2. Please back up any data if the tablet briefly powers on at any point, and remove any cases or accessories before sending it in.  
3. Once the repair center receives it, they’ll inspect and repair it; if it can’t be fixed, they may replace it according to the warranty terms.  

If you don’t see the email within 10–15 minutes, check your spam/junk folder or tell me and I can resend the instructions.

arguments.issue_description: Tablet (SlateTab Pro 11-inch) will not charge or power on at all despite trying multiple outlets and a different USB-C cable. No physical damage, no cracks, dents, or liquid exposure reported. Issue confirmed to be with the tablet hardware, not the charger.

conversation.content: Perfect, thanks for getting that set up under my extended warranty. I’ll watch for the email with the shipping instructions and label, and if it doesn’t show up in the next 15 minutes I’ll reach back out. [TASK_DONE]
